result.markdown:
--- title: "Nestlé S.A. ESG Governance & Climate Accountability Analysis" description: "Advanced financial analysis of corporate governance and sustainability targets using Python." author: "SONTING CHEN" date: "2025-04-20" categories: ["ESG", "Corporate Governance", "Python Analysis"] jupyter: python3 format: html: # 1. 布局核心设置 page-layout: article max-width: 1050px # 2. 交互式目录 (TOC) toc: true toc-depth: 3 toc-location: left toc-title: "Report Menu" # 3. 代码块控制 code-fold: true code-summary: "View Python Source Code" code-tools: true theme: - cosmo highlight-style: github fig-cap-location: bottom smooth-scroll: true css: | body { background-color: #fcfcfc; } .quarto-title { border-bottom: 2px solid #2E86AB; padding-bottom: 10px; } #title-block-header { margin-top: 3rem; margin-bottom: 3rem; } --- **Academic Project 2024–2025 | DCU Accounting & Finance** ## Executive Summary This project analyses Nestlé’s corporate governance structure, ESG accountability, climate risk management, and sustainability performance using real-world data from the 2024 Non-Financial Statement, Corporate Governance Report, and public audits. Using Python, we visualise: - Board composition & independence - ESG-linked executive remuneration - Plastic reduction target performance - Governance accountability gaps This analysis applies **Agency Theory** and **Stakeholder Theory** to evaluate governance effectiveness in delivering genuine climate action. --- ## 1. Import Python Libraries ::: {#6e40e08c .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import plotly.express as px # Optimize global plotting style for high-end aesthetics (differentiated from Project 1) plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-white') plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'Inter' # Unified professional font plt.rcParams['axes.facecolor'] = '#fafafa' # Soft neutral background plt.rcParams['axes.edgecolor'] = '#e0e0e0' # Subtle border plt.rcParams['axes.labelweight'] = 'bold' # Bold axis labels for clarity ``` ::: * **System Setup**: Environment configured with Pandas for data processing and Matplotlib/Plotly for professional financial visualisations. --- ## 2. ESG & Governance KPIs (Nestlé 2024) ::: {#2a56ab38 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Core governance and sustainability metrics (structured for clarity) metrics = [ "Board Independence (%)", "ESG in Annual Bonus (%)", "LTI Linked to Climate (%)", "Plastic Reduction Target Achieved (%)", "Plastic Reduction Goal (%)" ] values = [92, 15, 20, 21.3, 33] # Board structure (enhanced data for richer visualization) board_data = { "Category": ["Total Directors", "Independent Directors", "Female Directors", "Male Directors"], "Count": [13, 12, 4, 9], "Color": ["#2E86AB", "#A23B72", "#F18F01", "#3C6E71"] # Custom color mapping } # Executive incentive breakdown (detailed for advanced visualization) incentive_data = { 'Component': ['Base Salary', 'Annual Bonus (ESG Linked)', 'Long-Term Incentive (Climate)'], 'Weight (%)': [60, 15, 20], 'Description': ['Fixed compensation', 'Tied to ESG performance metrics', 'Linked to climate targets'] } ``` ::: * **Data Foundation**: KPIs derived from Nestlé’s 2024 Corporate Governance and Non-Financial Statements, focusing on board structure and incentive alignment. --- ## 3. Visualise ESG & Governance Performance ::: {#cell-fig-radar .cell fig-height='5' fig-width='5' execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import numpy as np # Suppress font warnings plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'sans-serif' plt.rcParams['font.sans-serif'] = ['Arial', 'DejaVu Sans', 'sans-serif'] # Prepare Radar Chart data num_vars = len(metrics) angles = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, num_vars, endpoint=False).tolist() values_radar = values + values[:1] # Close the loop angles += angles[:1] # Reduced figsize (5x5) for a more compact radar chart fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 5), subplot_kw=dict(polar=True)) # Draw the radar plot ax.fill(angles, values_radar, color='#2E86AB', alpha=0.25) ax.plot(angles, values_radar, color='#2E86AB', linewidth=1.5, marker='o', markersize=4) # Professional styling ax.set_theta_offset(np.pi / 2) ax.set_theta_direction(-1) plt.xticks(angles[:-1], metrics, fontsize=8) # Smaller font for cleaner look ax.set_rlabel_position(0) plt.yticks([25, 50, 75, 100], ["25%", "50%", "75%", "100%"], color="grey", size=7) plt.ylim(0, 100) plt.title('ESG Performance Radar (2024)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', pad=20) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](project2_nestle_esg_governance_files/figure-html/fig-radar-output-1.png){#fig-radar width=476 height=377} ::: ::: * **Key Insight**: Nestlé shows exceptional Board Independence (92%), providing a strong foundation for oversight. * **Observation**: There is a visible skew towards governance structure over environmental target achievement. --- ## 4. Board Composition Analysis ::: {#cell-fig-treemap .cell fig-height='3.5' fig-width='6' execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import plotly.express as px df_board = pd.DataFrame(board_data) # Reduced size for a more sophisticated web appearance fig_tree = px.treemap(df_board, path=['Category'], values='Count', title='Board Composition Hierarchy', color='Count', color_continuous_scale='RdBu', width=600, height=350) fig_tree.update_layout( title_font_size=14, title_font_weight='bold', margin=dict(t=40, l=10, r=10, b=10), plot_bgcolor='white', paper_bgcolor='white' ) fig_tree.show() ``` ::: {#fig-treemap .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: * **Key Insight**: Board independence is nearly absolute (12/13 directors), significantly minimizing agency costs and potential management entrenchment. * **Data Point**: Gender diversity stands at 31%, highlighting a secondary focus area for future governance evolution. --- ## 5. Plastic Reduction Target Gap Analysis ::: {#cell-fig-gap-analysis .cell fig-height='1.6' fig-width='7' execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} import matplotlib.pyplot as plt # Using a much slimmer height (1.6) for a refined dashboard appearance plt.figure(figsize=(7, 1.6)) # Suppress font warnings plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'sans-serif' plt.rcParams['font.sans-serif'] = ['Arial', 'DejaVu Sans', 'sans-serif'] # Data points categories = ['Plastic Reduction'] actual_val = 21.3 target_val = 33 # Plot target bar (background) and actual bar (foreground) plt.barh(categories, [target_val], color='#E0E0E0', label='Target (33%)', height=0.4) plt.barh(categories, [actual_val], color='#A23B72', label='Actual (21.3%)', height=0.2) # Add target vertical line plt.axvline(x=target_val, color='#C73E1D', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5) # Add value annotations plt.text(target_val + 0.5, 0, f'Target: {target_val}%', color='#C73E1D', va='center', fontweight='bold', fontsize=9) plt.text(actual_val - 0.8, 0, f'{actual_val}%', color='white', va='center', ha='right', fontweight='bold', fontsize=8) # Styling and minimalist cleanup plt.title('Progress Toward 2025 Sustainability Goal', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', pad=10) plt.xlabel('Reduction Percentage (%)', fontsize=9) plt.xlim(0, 45) plt.yticks([]) # Hide y-axis text to save space and look cleaner plt.legend(loc='lower right', fontsize=8, frameon=False) plt.grid(axis='x', linestyle=':', alpha=0.3) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](project2_nestle_esg_governance_files/figure-html/fig-gap-analysis-output-1.png){#fig-gap-analysis width=662 height=145} ::: ::: * **Key Insight**: An 11.7% gap currently exists between the 2024 performance (21.3%) and the 2025 plastic reduction goal (33%). * **Strategic Risk**: Accelerated investment in circular economy infrastructure is required to mitigate potential greenwashing accusations. --- ## 6. ESG Remuneration Structure ::: {#cell-fig-remuneration .cell fig-height='4' fig-width='6' execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import plotly.express as px df_inc = pd.DataFrame(incentive_data) # Reduced size for a professional dashboard look fig = px.pie(df_inc, values='Weight (%)', names='Component', title='Executive Remuneration Structure', hole=0.45, # Slightly larger hole for a more modern look color_discrete_map={ 'Base Salary': '#2E86AB', 'Annual Bonus (ESG Linked)': '#F18F01', 'Long-Term Incentive (Climate)': '#A23B72' }, width=550, height=380) # Enhance aesthetics and remove Inter font dependency fig.update_layout( title_font_size=13, title_font_weight='bold', margin=dict(t=50, l=10, r=10, b=10), plot_bgcolor='#fafafa', paper_bgcolor='white', legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.2, xanchor="center", x=0.5) ) # Optimize text and hover tooltips fig.update_traces( textposition='inside', textinfo='percent', hovertemplate='%{label}
Weight: %{value}%
%{customdata}', customdata=df_inc['Description'] ) fig.show() ``` ::: {#fig-remuneration .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: * **Key Insight**: 40% of executive variable pay is directly linked to ESG and Climate targets, aligning management incentives with long-term sustainability. * **Theoretical Alignment**: This structure utilizes **Agency Theory** to ensure executives prioritize environmental outcomes alongside financial returns. --- ## 7. Governance Analysis & Professional Insights ### Key Findings: 1. **Strong Governance Structure** - 92% independent directors (12/13) – exceeds industry average of 75% - Dedicated Sustainability Committee at board level, with quarterly ESG audits - ESG metrics integrated into executive pay (35% of variable remuneration linked to sustainability) 2. **Critical Accountability Gap** - Only **21.3% achieved** against a 33% plastic reduction target (2025 goal) - Governance design (policy, structure) exceeds operational delivery (execution, outcomes) - Tension between corporate lobbying (e.g., on packaging regulations) and public sustainability commitments 3. **Theoretical Framework** - **Agency Theory**: ESG KPIs align management incentives with shareholder climate risk management - **Stakeholder Theory**: Sustainability programs address environmental, social, and regulatory stakeholder demands - Hybrid model reveals implementation gaps between policy design and on-the-ground practice ### Recommendations: - Establish a dedicated **Climate Oversight Committee** (independent directors only) to monitor target progress - Allocate 30% of Capital Expenditure (CapEx) to decarbonisation and circular economy initiatives - Mandate board-level climate literacy training (aligned with TCFD recommendations) - Implement quarterly public disclosures on plastic reduction progress to enhance transparency --- ## 8. Technical Skills & Core Competencies - Data Visualisation: Advanced proficiency in Python (Pandas, Matplotlib, Plotly) for financial and ESG data storytelling. - Governance Analysis: Deep understanding of Agency Theory and Board Accountability frameworks - ESG Compliance: Applied knowledge of ISSB, CSRD, and TCFD sustainability reporting standards. - Financial Auditing: Quantitative synthesis of Corporate Governance Reports and Non-Financial Statements. - Strategic Modeling: Performed Gap Analysis for climate targets and executive remuneration alignment.
